Sin is the cause of our misery; but the transgressions of the true believer against the Divine law are all forgiven, because they are covered by the atonement. Christ bore their sins, therefore they are not held against them. Because Christ’s righteousness is reckoned to them, and they are made the righteousness of God in him, their iniquity is not held against them; God has laid upon him the iniquity of us all, and made him a sin-offering for us. Not holding sin accountable is God's act, because he is the Judge. God justifies. Notice the character of the person whose sins are pardoned; they are sincere, and seek holiness by the power of the Holy Spirit. They do not profess repentance with an intention to indulge in sin, because the Lord is ready to forgive. They will not abuse the doctrine of free grace. And to the person whose iniquity is forgiven, all kinds of blessings are promised.

It is very difficult to bring a sinful person humbly to accept free mercy, with a full confession of their sins and self-condemnation. But the true and only way to peace of conscience is to confess your sins that they may be forgiven; to declare them so you may be justified. Although repentance and confession do not earn the pardon of transgression, they are necessary for the real enjoyment of forgiving mercy. And what words can tell the happiness of that hour when the soul, oppressed by sin, is enabled freely to pour out its sorrows before God, and to take hold of his promised mercy in Christ Jesus! Those who want to be successful in prayer must seek the Lord when, by his providence, he calls them to seek him, and, by his Spirit, stirs them up to seek him. In a time of finding, when the heart is softened with grief, and burdened with guilt; when all human refuge fails; when no rest can be found for the troubled mind, then it is that God applies the healing balm by his Spirit.

God teaches by his word, and guides with the secret intimations of his will. David gives a warning to sinners. The reason for this warning is that the way of sin will certainly end in sorrow. Here is a word of comfort to saints. They may see that a life of communion with God is far the most pleasant and comfortable. Let us rejoice, O Lord Jesus, in you, and in your salvation; so shall we rejoice indeed.
